### Post by cisoprogressivo on 2006-11-23
i've to ask sorry for my english, but i think that this is the only forum that can help me.
I've a ATI Xpress 200M with 24.8 driver installed.
My monitor does not power off!
But only became black...
Are there someone who can help me?
This is my xorg.conf
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig Screen 0" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"

EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts

	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"

	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"

EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig Monitor 0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Xpress 200M (RS480)"

	BusID       "PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Graphics Adapter 0"

	Option	    "(null)"
	Option	    "VideoOverlay" "on"
	Option	    "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
	BusID       "PCI:1:5:0"
	Option "no_accel" "no"
	Option "no_dri" "no"

	Option "mtrr" "on"
```

### Post by croto on 2006-11-24
Hi,

did you try to switch it off with "xset dpms force standby" on the command line? That should put your monitor to sleep.

If that works, it might be just a problem in the power management  configuration....

Hope it helps.

---

Hope it helps.

---

### Post by croto on 2006-11-25
just open a terminal (Applications -> Accesories -> Terminal) and type:

xset dpms force standby

Did it do anything?

---

### Post by croto on 2006-11-25
What do you mean by black? Is it exactly as if the monitor is off but the power led is on, or the screen goes black while the monitor still emits light? 

do you have a laptop or a desktop computer? If you have a desktop, what happens if you switch the monitor off using the on-off switch?

do you have a laptop or a desktop computer? If you have a desktop, what happens if you switch the monitor off using the on-off switch?

thanks for the patience. :) 
i means that the screen goes black while the monitor still emits light (so when i go to sleep, the computer still emits light)...
i have a laptop, an HP Pavilion zv6000

---

### Post by croto on 2006-11-25
Try this. In your xorg.conf, you have this section:

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig Monitor 0"
EndSection

Add one line so it looks like:

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig Monitor 0"
        Option "DPMS"
EndSection

And restart X11 (log out, and then hit CTRL-ALT-BACKSPACE). Then try that "xset dpms force standby", and tell me if that helps.

---

### Post by cisoprogressivo on 2006-11-25
> **croto said:**
> Try this. In your xorg.conf, you have this section:

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig Monitor 0"
EndSection

Add one line so it looks like:

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig Monitor 0"
        Option "DPMS"
EndSection

And restart X11 (log out, and then hit CTRL-ALT-BACKSPACE). Then try that "xset dpms force standby", and tell me if that helps.

"thanks!!!"
you've solved a troble that nobody can solve in the italian ubuntu forum :D :D :D :D :D

### Post by croto on 2006-11-25
Check the "Power Management" options (System -> Preferences -> Power Management". Is it set to put the display to sleep after some time of inactivity? I have Edgy, I guess it should be the same in Dapper...

### Post by Supersaiyan.IV on 2006-12-13
I have the same problem, meaning the screen just goes blank, and does not go to sleep. However the DPMS command doesn't help!
Maybe display sleep is unsupported under XGL.. dunno really..

> 
kuba@MATRIX-A1:~$ sudo xset +dpms
kuba@MATRIX-A1:~$ sudo xset dpms force standby
X Error of failed request:  BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes)
  Major opcode of failed request:  146 (DPMS)
  Minor opcode of failed request:  6 (DPMSForceLevel)
  Serial number of failed request:  10
  Current serial number in output stream:  12
kuba@MATRIX-A1:~$ 


This only happens in XGL. Screensavers work, but power management doesn't.
Adding dpms did not change anything either :/ Now this is a very mildly configured system, practically only FGLRX, XGL and beryl on it.
Anya help is greatly appreciated!
here's the xorg.conf:

> 
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"

	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"

	# path to defoma fonts

EndSection

Section "Module"

	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"

	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"

                                                      # for USB

  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY

Section "Monitor"

### Post by croto on 2006-12-14
did you try this?

[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=282229](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=282229)

I'm not using Xgl, so I can't try it. But they say that when you use Xgl,  the standard Xorg display is :0, so I'd see if this works:

DISPLAY=:0 xset dpms force standby
